name: SAP Analytics Cloud
tagline: SAS wins because we deliver purpose-built analytical depth and production decisioning that SAP's bolt-on analytics cannot match

overview.whatTheyDo: SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) is SAP's unified BI, planning, and predictive analytics platform in the cloud. Combines business intelligence (dashboards, stories), enterprise planning (financial planning, budgeting), and augmented analytics (Smart Predict, Smart Insights). Part of the broader SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) which includes SAP HANA Cloud, SAP Datasphere, and SAP AI Core. SAP positions SAC as the analytics layer for SAP-centric enterprises.
overview.sweetSpot: Organizations deeply embedded in SAP ERP (S/4HANA, ECC) wanting unified reporting/planning on their SAP data without ETL to external tools. Finance teams needing integrated planning + reporting. SAP-centric IT departments wanting to consolidate on SAP's stack.
overview.typicalBuyer: CFO (planning), CIO/CTO (SAP stack consolidation), VP Finance, Head of Enterprise Applications, SAP Center of Excellence leads
overview.phPresence: Strong in PH enterprises running SAP ERP — major conglomerates (SM, Ayala, JG Summit, San Miguel, Aboitiz), some banks (RCBC, PNB), and manufacturing. SAP Philippines (Makati) actively promotes SAC to existing ERP customers. Partner ecosystem: Accenture PH, IBM PH, Hitachi Solutions, local SAP partners (Dion Global, IDCP). SAP ERP is deeply embedded in PH large enterprise — SAC rides that installed base.
overview.recentMomentum: SAP Datasphere (data fabric), SAP AI Core (ML ops), Joule (GenAI copilot), deeper S/4HANA Cloud integration, SAC embedded analytics within SAP applications. SAP pushing 'RISE with SAP' and 'GROW with SAP' cloud ERP migrations — bundling SAC. Smart Predict improvement for automated forecasting.
overview.pricingModel: Subscription per user: Business Intelligence ~$22/user/month, Planning Professional ~$36/user/month. Often bundled into SAP enterprise agreements (RISE with SAP includes SAC BI). Volume discounts for existing SAP customers. Can appear 'free' when bundled with ERP — similar dynamic to Power BI in M365.

whereWeWin.headline: Statistical & ML Depth Beyond 'Augmented' Analytics
whereWeWin.explanation: SAS provides 200+ validated statistical and ML algorithms, visual model building, champion/challenger testing, and governed model deployment. SAP Smart Predict offers basic AutoML (classification, regression, time series) with limited algorithm choice, no transparency into model internals, and no formal governance workflow. For BSP 1112 model risk management, Smart Predict's black-box approach is insufficient.
whereWeWin.proofPoint: SAS Model Manager tracks full model lifecycle with regulatory documentation. SAP Smart Predict creates a model but provides no lifecycle management, no champion/challenger, and no drift detection — gaps that BSP examiners will flag.

whereWeWin.headline: Real-Time Fraud & Risk Decisioning (Purpose-Built)
whereWeWin.explanation: SAS delivers production-grade fraud detection (<10ms scoring), AML monitoring, and real-time risk decisioning specifically designed for BSP/AFASA compliance. SAP has no equivalent real-time fraud decisioning capability — SAP GRC handles audit/compliance workflows but not transaction-level real-time scoring. The BSP 1213 gap is fundamental.
whereWeWin.proofPoint: SAS processes billions of fraud transactions daily across 3,000+ institutions. SAP GRC manages compliance workflows but cannot score individual transactions in real-time.

whereWeWin.headline: Analytics Independence from ERP Vendor Lock-In
whereWeWin.explanation: SAS connects to ANY data source — SAP, Oracle, Salesforce, cloud databases, data lakes, mainframes. SAP Analytics Cloud is optimized for SAP data and struggles with non-SAP sources (limited connectors, Datasphere required for external data). As PH enterprises diversify beyond SAP ERP (cloud-native apps, digital channels, external data), SAP-only analytics becomes a constraint.
whereWeWin.proofPoint: SAS connects natively to 200+ data sources. SAP Analytics Cloud requires SAP Datasphere or manual data import for non-SAP data — adding cost and complexity for multi-source analytics.

whereTheyWin.area: Native SAP ERP Integration (Zero ETL for SAP Data)
whereTheyWin.whyItMatters: SAP Analytics Cloud connects directly to S/4HANA and ECC via live connection — no ETL, real-time SAP data in dashboards. For SAP-centric organizations, this means finance teams see real-time P&L, inventory, and procurement data without any data pipeline. In PH conglomerates running SAP, this 'zero effort' integration is compelling.
whereTheyWin.howToNeutralize: SAS connects to SAP via certified connectors (SAS/ACCESS Interface to SAP). The connection exists — it's just not 'native' like SAC. For SAP operational reporting (P&L, inventory, procurement), SAC is the right tool. For ANALYTICAL workloads (fraud, risk, forecasting, modeling) that span SAP AND non-SAP data, SAS is the right tool. Different purposes.

whereTheyWin.whyItMatters: SAP Analytics Cloud's planning module is genuinely strong — financial planning, budgeting, forecasting, workforce planning integrated with BI in one tool. This is unique — no other BI tool combines planning + analytics this tightly. PH CFOs running SAP see this as the single tool for finance.
whereTheyWin.howToNeutralize: SAS doesn't compete in FP&A/budgeting workflows. Concede this to SAP. SAS competes on ANALYTICAL forecasting (demand forecasting, risk forecasting, fraud prediction) — not budget planning. Different: SAP plans the budget, SAS predicts the fraud losses that impact the budget. Complementary.

whereTheyWin.whyItMatters: SAP bundles SAC into RISE with SAP cloud ERP contracts — making it 'free' for customers migrating to S/4HANA Cloud. PH enterprises currently in RISE migration waves (Ayala, SM, others) get SAC included. Hard to sell against 'free.'
whereTheyWin.howToNeutralize: Same playbook as Power BI: don't compete with free for basic BI/reporting. Position SAS for analytical workloads BEYOND what SAC covers — fraud, risk, modeling, decisioning. 'SAC handles your SAP reporting. SAS handles your regulated analytics. Both are needed. One is free. The other prevents regulatory penalties and fraud losses worth millions.'

features.notes: SAC has live connection to S/4HANA; SAS via certified connector (works but not native)
SAC planning module is market-leading; SAS doesn't compete in FP&A
SAS 200+ algorithms; SAP Smart Predict basic AutoML only
SAS Model Manager purpose-built; SAP has no model lifecycle management
SAS Intelligent Decisioning <10ms; SAP has no equivalent real-time scoring
SAS production solutions; SAP GRC is audit/compliance, not real-time detection
SAS connects to anything; SAC optimized for SAP data, limited non-SAP connectors
Both capable; SAC slightly better for SAP data visualization
SAS deeper model choice; SAP Smart Predict basic classification/regression/time series
SAS Forecasting handles millions of time series; SAC predictive forecasting limited scope
SAS native NLP/text analytics; SAP has Joule GenAI but no text analytics
SAS purpose-built for regulated industries; SAP GRC is audit workflow, not analytical compliance

landmines.question: What analytical capabilities do you use for data OUTSIDE your SAP system — digital channels, social media, external risk data, third-party fraud signals?
landmines.expectedAnswer: SAC struggles with non-SAP data — they may admit using separate tools or manual imports
landmines.whatToSayNext: Modern analytics requires multi-source data — not just ERP transactions. Fraud detection combines SAP transaction data WITH digital behavior, device signals, and external watchlists. SAS connects to ALL sources natively. SAC is optimized for SAP data only — limiting your analytical scope.

landmines.question: How does Smart Predict handle model validation, approval workflows, and documentation for BSP examination?
landmines.expectedAnswer: Smart Predict creates models but has no governance workflow — no approval chains, no regulatory documentation
landmines.whatToSayNext: BSP Circular 1112 requires documented model lifecycle — development, validation, approval, monitoring, retirement. SAS Model Manager provides this end-to-end. Smart Predict is 'push-button ML' without the governance BSP expects.

landmines.question: Can SAP Analytics Cloud detect a fraudulent transaction in real-time and trigger an automatic blocking action?
landmines.expectedAnswer: No — SAC is a reporting/planning tool, not a real-time decisioning engine
landmines.whatToSayNext: BSP 1213 requires real-time fraud monitoring and automated response by June 2026. SAP GRC handles compliance workflows AFTER the fact. SAS detects and blocks fraud IN REAL-TIME. These are fundamentally different capabilities.

objections.objection: We're standardizing on SAP for everything — SAP Analytics Cloud covers our analytics needs
objections.whyTheySayIt: CIO wants vendor consolidation on SAP. SAP sales pushes 'one stack' narrative. RISE with SAP includes SAC. Path of least resistance.
objections.response: SAP is excellent for ERP and operational reporting. But 'standardize on SAP for everything' has limits. Ask: can SAC detect fraud in real-time? Build and govern ML models for BSP? Monitor AML transactions? Forecast demand across millions of SKUs? These are purpose-built analytical capabilities that SAP acknowledges are outside their core. That's why SAP partners with companies like SAS — SAP for ERP and operations, specialized platforms for regulated analytics. Vendor consolidation shouldn't mean capability compromise.
proofPoints: SAP themselves recommend partners for fraud/risk analytics — they know SAC doesn't cover this
BSP doesn't accept 'we standardized on SAP' as excuse for missing fraud monitoring capability
Many SAP shops globally run SAS for regulated analytics alongside SAP for ERP — proven coexistence
objections.whatNotToSay: Don't attack SAP as a company — PH enterprises have deep SAP relationships and trust. Position SAS as complementary to SAP, not competitive with SAP ERP.

objections.objection: SAP Smart Predict and SAP AI Core handle our ML/AI needs
objections.whyTheySayIt: SAP marketing pushes AI capabilities. Customer hasn't tested depth. SAP account team selling AI features.
objections.response: Smart Predict provides basic AutoML — useful for simple forecasting within SAC dashboards. SAP AI Core provides ML operations infrastructure. But neither provides: 200+ validated algorithms with interpretability, governed model lifecycle with BSP 1112 compliance, champion/challenger testing with automatic rollback, real-time decisioning combining rules + ML, or pre-built industry models for fraud/AML/risk. For regulated ML in banking and insurance, you need purpose-built governance that SAP doesn't offer. SAS has spent 50 years building exactly this.
proofPoints: Smart Predict offers 3 model types (classification, regression, time series); SAS offers 200+
SAP AI Core is infrastructure — like Kubernetes for ML. SAS is complete solution with algorithms + governance + deployment
No BSP-regulated bank in ASEAN uses Smart Predict as their model governance platform
objections.whatNotToSay: Don't say 'SAP AI is bad' — it's fine for simple use cases within SAP workflows. Say 'SAP AI is basic' and show the governance depth gap for regulated industries.

phContext.localPresence: Strong PH presence — SAP Philippines office in Makati. SAC promoted to all SAP ERP customers during RISE/GROW migrations. Partner ecosystem: Accenture PH (largest SAP partner), IBM PH, Hitachi Solutions, Dion Global, IDCP. SAP is deeply embedded in PH large enterprise — virtually every conglomerate runs SAP. SAC rides this installed base aggressively.
knownCustomers: Ayala Group (SAP S/4HANA migration, SAC included)
San Miguel Corporation (SAP ERP, large data volumes)
Select manufacturing companies (Nestlé PH, P&G PH via global SAP)
phContext.localPricing: SAC BI ~$22/user/month, Planning ~$36/user/month. Often bundled 'free' in RISE with SAP contracts — similar dynamic to Power BI in M365. PH enterprises migrating to S/4HANA Cloud get SAC included. Volume discounts through SAP EA. Perceived as zero incremental cost for existing SAP customers.
regulatoryGaps: No real-time fraud detection or transaction scoring (BSP 1213 gap)
No model lifecycle governance for BSP 1112 compliance
Smart Predict too basic for regulated model building — no interpretability, no approval workflow
No AML/CFT monitoring or suspicious activity reporting (AMLA gap)
SAP GRC is audit/compliance workflow — NOT analytical fraud/risk detection
Limited non-SAP data connectivity restricts multi-source fraud analytics

partnerWhen: Customer is deep SAP shop — SAC handles their SAP operational reporting (let it)
Planning/FP&A is the primary SAC use case — SAS doesn't compete here
Position SAS for regulated analytics (fraud, risk, ML governance) alongside SAC for SAP BI
Different user segments: SAC for finance/operations, SAS for analytics/risk/compliance teams
competeWhen: SAP positioning Smart Predict as sufficient for regulated ML needs
Customer evaluating SAC as complete replacement for enterprise analytics platform
Primary need is advanced analytics/ML (not SAP reporting or planning)
Customer analytics scope extends significantly beyond SAP ERP data

proofPoints.title: PH conglomerate: SAP ERP + SAS Analytics coexistence
proofPoints.detail: Major Philippine conglomerate runs SAP S/4HANA for operations and SAS for fraud analytics, demand forecasting, and risk management — proving SAP + SAS complementary architecture in PH enterprise.

proofPoints.title: Gartner: SAP not positioned in AI/ML or Fraud Management
proofPoints.detail: Gartner positions SAP in ERP and Planning, not in AI/ML platforms or Enterprise Fraud Management — confirming SAP Analytics Cloud is BI/Planning, not advanced analytics.

proofPoints.title: Smart Predict vs. SAS: Governance depth comparison
proofPoints.detail: SAP Smart Predict offers 3 model types with no governance workflow. SAS provides 200+ algorithms with full lifecycle management, approval workflows, and BSP-grade documentation — purpose-built for regulated industries.

proofPoints.title: Global manufacturer: SAP for planning, SAS for supply chain analytics
proofPoints.detail: Fortune 500 manufacturer uses SAP Analytics Cloud for financial planning and budgeting, and SAS for demand forecasting, supply chain optimization, and quality analytics — each platform in its strength area.
